## 8. Decision framework: choosing a sequencing strategy

Use these questions before choosing a technology:

```{mermaid}
%%{init: {"themeVariables": {"fontSize": "10px"}, "flowchart": {"padding": 4, "nodeSpacing": 8, "rankSpacing": 14, "diagramPadding": 2}}}%%
flowchart LR
  A["**What biological variation matters most?**"] --> B["**Variation types** SNPs, indels, SVs repeats, methylation isoforms, mobile elements, copy number, haplotypes?"]
```

```{mermaid}
%%{init: {"themeVariables": {"fontSize": "10px"}, "flowchart": {"padding": 4, "nodeSpacing": 8, "rankSpacing": 14, "diagramPadding": 2}}}%%
flowchart LR
  A["**What scale is required?**"] --> B["**Project scale**  One reference-quality genome tens of assemblies thousands of genotyped samples or environmental mixtures?"]
```

```{mermaid}
%%{init: {"themeVariables": {"fontSize": "10px"}, "flowchart": {"padding": 4, "nodeSpacing": 8, "rankSpacing": 14, "diagramPadding": 2}}}%%
flowchart LR
  A["**What molecule quality is realistic?**"] --> B["**Available material** High-molecular-weight DNA degraded DNA, RNA low-input or single cells ancient DNA?"]
```

```{mermaid}
%%{init: {"themeVariables": {"fontSize": "10px"}, "flowchart": {"padding": 4, "nodeSpacing": 8, "rankSpacing": 14, "diagramPadding": 2}}}%%
flowchart LR
  A["**What representation will be used downstream?**"] --> B["**Representation** Linear reference, assembly set variation or pangenome graph colored de Bruijn graph or presence/absence matrix?"]
```

```{mermaid}
%%{init: {"themeVariables": {"fontSize": "10px"}, "flowchart": {"padding": 4, "nodeSpacing": 8, "rankSpacing": 14, "diagramPadding": 2}}}%%
flowchart LR
  A["**What is the acceptable error model?**"] --> B["**Errors and biases** Random or systematic errors homopolymer errors, GC bias mapping bias or allele dropout?"]
```

```{mermaid}
%%{init: {"themeVariables": {"fontSize": "10px"}, "flowchart": {"padding": 4, "nodeSpacing": 8, "rankSpacing": 14, "diagramPadding": 2}}}%%
flowchart LR
  A["**What information must be preserved?**"] --> B["**Information** Raw signal, base modifications barcodes, molecule identity read group metadata or phase blocks?"]
```

```{mermaid}
%%{init: {"themeVariables": {"fontSize": "10px"}, "flowchart": {"padding": 4, "nodeSpacing": 8, "rankSpacing": 14, "diagramPadding": 2}}}%%
flowchart LR
  A["**What computational resources are available?**"] --> B["**Resources** Storage, CPU/GPU, memory workflow reproducibility and long-term archiving?"]
```

### Rule of thumb

- Use **short reads** when scale and cost matter most and the target variation is already well represented.
- Use **HiFi long reads** when accurate assembly and sequence-level SV resolution are central.
- Use **ultra-long nanopore reads** when molecule length and native signal matter most.
- Use **Hi-C/optical/linked-read data** when long-range structure, scaffolding, or phasing is required.
- Use **RNA and epigenomic data** when annotation and function matter, not only sequence structure.

---
